mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that raises discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% indicates a tight labor market, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where RSAFS and HOUST both rise, or interaction effect size < 0.15 in rolling 5-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 102.5 signals weak supply chain capacity, creating inflationary pressures that depress S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where INDPRO < 102.5, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in CPI inflation below 3% indicates deflationary risk, prompting central bank easing that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL < 3%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k signals robust labor market growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 45% of instances where PAYEMS > 150k, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent drop in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 40% of instances where GDPC1 and HOUST both decline, or interaction effect size < 0.12 in rolling 5-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 103 combined with falling unemployment (UNRATE) indicates overheating supply-side capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO > 103 and UNRATE < 3.6%, or interaction effect size < 0.10 in rolling 5-year regressions.
